steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The second team concluded that Kozak & Keoleian's study had more specificity in its data than Oguchi's. The passage tells us that a higher detail rating means "reported data with more detail" — data specificity is measured by the detail rating. A finding that challenges the conclusion must contradict the idea that Kozak & Keoleian had more specific data.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "The study by Kozak and Keoleian had a lower detail rating than the study by Oguchi and colleagues did.": A lower detail rating means less detailed (less specific) data for Kozak & Keoleian — directly contradicting the conclusion that their data had more specificity. ✓
  • Choice B — "The study by Kozak and Keoleian had a lower traceability rating than the study by Oguchi and colleagues did.": Traceability measures clarity of procedures, not data specificity. A lower traceability rating does not challenge a claim about data detail. ✗
  • Choice C — "The study by Kozak and Keoleian had a high detail rating and a high traceability rating.": High detail and high traceability would support the conclusion, not challenge it. ✗
  • Choice D — "The study by Oguchi and colleagues had a low detail rating and a low traceability rating.": If Oguchi's ratings were low, Kozak & Keoleian would comparatively look better, which supports rather than challenges the conclusion. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
